hypothesis: Training a coupled ensemble consensus composed of heterogeneous GNN architectures (e.g., PaiNN, GIN, and GatedGCN) will produce individual deployed models that, under a fixed total parameter and compute budget, achieve higher out-of-distribution test accuracy and better calibration (lower NLL and ECE) on an external unlabeled-to-labeled molecular dataset than (i) homogeneous coupled ensembles of identical architecture and (ii) standard decoupled ensembles.
